post.description:
Sign up endpoint - Backend handles everything.

Frontend sends: email, password, display_name
Backend:
1. Creates user in Firebase
2. Gets Firebase ID token
3. Creates user in our database
4. Returns token + user info

No Authorization header needed!

Login endpoint - Backend handles everything.

Frontend sends: email, password
Backend:
1. Authenticates with Firebase
2. Gets Firebase ID token
3. Gets/creates user in our database
4. Returns token + user info

No Authorization header needed!

Forgot password endpoint - Sends password reset email via Firebase.

Frontend sends: email
Backend:
1. Validates email format
2. Sends password reset email via Firebase
3. Firebase handles the reset link and email delivery
4. Returns success response

No Authorization header needed!

Note: Firebase will send a password reset email to the user's email address.
The email contains a link that allows the user to reset their password.
The link expires after 1 hour.

get.description:
Get current authenticated user's information.
Requires valid Firebase authentication token.

put.description:
Update current authenticated user's information.
Requires valid Firebase authentication token.

IMPORTANT: This only updates the user's info in our database, NOT in Firebase.
- Updating email in Firebase requires reauthentication and should be done client-side
- display_name can be safely updated here
- For email changes: User must update email in Firebase first (client-side), then update here

delete.description:
Delete current authenticated user's account.
Requires valid Firebase authentication token.

Note: This only deletes the user from our database.
The Firebase account should be deleted separately.

post.description:
Google Sign-In endpoint - Backend handles Firebase authentication.

Frontend sends: Google ID token (obtained from Google Sign-In SDK)
Backend:
1. Exchanges Google ID token for Firebase token
2. Gets user info from Firebase
3. Creates/updates user in our database
4. Returns Firebase token + user info

No Authorization header needed!

Refresh an expired Firebase ID token using a refresh token.

Frontend sends: refresh_token
Backend:
1. Exchanges refresh token for a new ID token with Firebase
2. Gets user info from our database
3. Returns new token + user info

No Authorization header needed!

get.description:
Verify that the provided Firebase token is valid.
Useful for checking authentication status.
Get current user's preferences (language, custom prompt, response style).

put.description:
Update user's preferences (language, custom prompt, response style).

- language_preference: "auto", "Arabic", "English", etc.
- custom_system_prompt: Additional modifier appended to the default system prompt.
- response_preferences: Dict with {length, style, focus}

get.description:
Get current user's mode preference.

Returns:
    mode_preference: 0 (default/auto), 1 (research mode), 2 (fact check mode)

put.description:
Update current user's mode preference.

Args:
    mode_preference: 0 (default/auto), 1 (research mode), 2 (fact check mode)

Returns:
    Updated user data with new mode preference

post.description:
PUBLIC guest chat endpoint - NO authentication required.

Features:
- No signup/login required
- Rate limited per IP address (10 requests per minute)
- Session-based conversation tracking (client-side)
- No database storage
- Real-time token streaming
- Optional chain-of-thought thinking

Rate Limits:
- 10 requests per minute per IP address
- Returns 429 Too Many Requests if exceeded

Returns: text/event-stream with chunks as they generate

PUBLIC guest OCR endpoint - NO authentication required.

This endpoint allows guest users to extract text from images for review
before fact-checking. Rate limited per IP address.

Rate Limits:
- 5 requests per minute per IP address

Request:
{
    "image_base64": "base64_string",
    "session_id": "guest_uuid (optional)",
    "language_hint": "Arabic|English (optional)"
}

Response:
{
    "extracted_text": "النص المستخرج...",
    "image_url": "temp_key",
    "metadata": {...}
}

get.description: Public health check for guest endpoints.
Public health check endpoint - no authentication required.

post.description:
Protected chat endpoint - requires Firebase authentication.

Features:
- Language preference hierarchy: request > conversation > user > auto-detect
- Custom system prompts (modifiers, not replacements)
- Auto-generated AI conversation titles for new threads

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

Protected STREAMING chat endpoint - returns Server-Sent Events (SSE).

Features:
- Real-time token streaming
- Cancellation support (client disconnect detection)
- Language preference hierarchy: request > conversation > user > auto-detect
- Custom system prompts (modifiers, not replacements)
- Auto-generated AI conversation titles for new threads

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

Returns: text/event-stream with chunks as they generate

Generate an AI-powered title for a conversation based on the first question.

Request Body:
- question: str (required) - The first question in the conversation
- language: str (optional) - Language for the title ("Arabic" or "English", default: "Arabic")

Returns:
- title: str - AI-generated concise title

OCR endpoint for reviewing extracted text before fact-checking.

This is step 1 of the fact-check flow:
1. Frontend sends image → This endpoint
2. Backend saves image + extracts text via OCR
3. Returns extracted text + image URL
4. Frontend shows review UI (user can edit text)
5. User confirms → Frontend sends to /chat/stream with edited text

Request:
{
    "image_base64": "base64_string",
    "thread_id": "uuid (optional)",
    "language_hint": "Arabic|English (optional)"
}

Response:
{
    "extracted_text": "النص المستخرج...",
    "image_url": "https://shamela-storage.s3.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/...",
    "metadata": {
        "success": true,
        "detected_language": "Arabic",
        "confidence": "high",
        "text_length": 523
    }
}

Confirm reviewed text and automatically fact-check it.

This is step 2 of the improved fact-check flow:
1. User uploads image → /chat/ocr extracts text
2. User reviews/edits extracted text
3. User confirms → This endpoint automatically fact-checks

Flow:
- Backend automatically engineers the prompt
- Backend automatically sets conversation_mode="fact_check"
- User just confirms the text, no manual prompt needed

Request:
{
    "reviewed_text": "User-confirmed text",
    "image_url": "/uploads/...",
    "thread_id": "uuid (optional)",
    "language_preference": "Arabic|English (optional)",
    "enable_thinking": true
}

Response: SSE stream with fact-check results

get.description:
List all conversations for authenticated user. Protected endpoint.

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

post.description:
Create a new conversation. Protected endpoint.

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

delete.description:
Delete all conversations for authenticated user. Protected endpoint.

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

get.description:
Return all messages for a conversation. Protected endpoint.
Only the conversation owner can access it.

Query Parameters:
- auto_presign: Automatically generate pre-signed URLs for images (default: True)
- expiration: Pre-signed URL expiration in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour)

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

delete.description:
Delete a specific conversation. Protected endpoint.
Only the conversation owner can delete it.

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

get.description:
Get the share status of a conversation. Protected endpoint.
Only the conversation owner can check share status.

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

put.description:
Toggle sharing for a conversation. Protected endpoint.
Only the conversation owner can share/unshare.

Request Body:
- is_shared: true to make publicly viewable, false to make private

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

get.description:
Generate a pre-signed URL for accessing a private S3 image (GET method).

Protected endpoint - requires Firebase authentication.

Query Parameters:
- s3_url: Full S3 URL or S3 key (e.g., "uploads/2024/01/15/uuid.png")
- expiration: Optional expiration time in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour, max: 604800 = 7 days)

Returns:
- presigned_url: Temporary URL with access to the image
- expires_in: Expiration time in seconds

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

post.description:
Generate a pre-signed URL for accessing a private S3 image (POST method).

Protected endpoint - requires Firebase authentication.

Request Body:
- s3_url: Full S3 URL or S3 key (e.g., "uploads/2024/01/15/uuid.png")
- expiration: Optional expiration time in seconds (default: 3600 = 1 hour, max: 604800 = 7 days)

Returns:
- presigned_url: Temporary URL with access to the image
- expires_in: Expiration time in seconds

Headers required:
- Authorization: Bearer <firebase_id_token>

get.description:
Get a shared conversation's title and messages.

PUBLIC endpoint - no authentication required.
Only returns data if the conversation exists AND is_shared=TRUE.
